zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan De Beaumont於1321年出生在英國斯塔福爾時，她的父親Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris51歲，母親Alice Comyn32歲。她於大約1340年與Sir Fulk FitzWarin在英國英格蘭史洛普爾史福斯波利結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1349年7月25日在英國史洛普爾奧斯維斯崔享年28歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan De Beaumont于1321年出生在英国斯塔福爾时，她的父亲Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris51岁，母亲Alice Comyn32岁。她于大约1340年与Sir Fulk FitzWarin在英国英格兰史洛普爾史福斯波利结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1349年7月25日在英国史洛普爾奧斯維斯崔享年28岁。

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan De Beaumont was born in 1321, in Staffordshire, England, her father, Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris, was 51 and her mother, Alice Comyn, was 32. She married Sir Fulk FitzWarin about 1340, in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died on 25 July 1349, in Oswestry, Shropshire, England, at the age of 28.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan De Beaumont est née en 1321, à Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris, avait 51 ans et sa mère, Alice Comyn, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Fulk FitzWarin environ 1340, à Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée le 25 juillet 1349, à Oswestry, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 28 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan De Beaumont 1321 in Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris 51 und ihre Mutter Alice Comyn 32. Sie hat um 1340 in Shrewsbury, Shropshire, England, United Kingdom Sir Fulk FitzWarin geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb am 25. Juli 1349 im Alter von 28 Jahren in Oswestry, Shropshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan De Beaumont è nata il nel 1321 nel Staffordshire, England, sua padre, Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris, aveva 51 anni e sua madre, Alice Comyn, aveva 32 anni. Lei sposato Sir Fulk FitzWarin circa 1340, nel Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì il 25 luglio 1349, nel Oswestry, Shropshire, England, all'età di 28 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan De Beaumontが1321年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアで生まれた時，父Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxorisは51歳，母Alice Comynは32歳であった。約1340年にイギリスイギリスシュロプシュアイアシュリュースブリーでSir Fulk FitzWarinと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1349年7月25日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアオスウエストリーで28歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan De Beaumont(이)가 1321년 영국 스태퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris(은)는 51세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Comyn(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 대략 1340년 영국 잉글랜드 슈럽실르 슈레w스버이에서 Sir Fulk FitzWarin(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1349년 7월 25일 영국 슈럽실르 오스w스트리에서 28세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan De Beaumont nasceu em 1321, em Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris, tinha 51 anos e sua mãe, Alice Comyn, tinha 32 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Fulk FitzWarin aproximadamente 1340, em Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela faleceu em 25 de julho de 1349, em Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 28 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan De Beaumont родилась в 1321 г. в местности стаффордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris было 51 год, а матери, которую звали Alice Comyn, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Fulk FitzWarin около 1340 г. в местности шрюсбури, шропшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла 25 июля 1349 г. в местности освестри, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 28 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan De Beaumont nació en 1321, en Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris, tenía 51 años y su mamá, Alice Comyn, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Sir Fulk FitzWarin aproximadamente 1340, en Shrewsbury, Shropshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió el 25 de julio de 1349, en Oswestry, Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 28 años.
